Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Sep-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ross A. Spector for and with James R. McKenzie, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jennifer Beerline for Charles McKenzie, Respondent (via Zoom).
Martin Woods for and with Henry McKenzie (via Zoom).

Mr. Spector stated that settlement agreement needs to be signed, that there are about 50 documents to be approved, and that William McKenzie's successor interest are Matthew McKenzie and James McKenzie. The matter is continued to September 23, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Ng. Not reported.

Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ross A. Spector for and with James R. McKenzie, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jennifer Beerline for Charles McKenzie, Respondent (via Zoom).
Martin Woods for and with Henry McKenzie (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the settlement agreement, and it is largely completed they have to go through 60 exhibits. Parties agreed to a three-week continuance to resolve the outstanding issues.

Order to appear is ordered off calendar.

The matter is continued to July 23, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Off Calendar

Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Jun-25-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ross A. Spector for and with James R. McKenzie, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jennifer Beerline for Charles McKenzie, Respondent (via Zoom).

Mr. Spector informed the Court that the parties had been engaged in ongoing settlement discussions for nearly two years, including a mediation conducted by Judge Jacobs-May, where a settlement was reached approximately one year ago. However, finalizing the settlement had stalled. He noted that over 50 documents were involved and that significant estate funds had already been expended toward the settlement effort. The procedural issues raised in the examiner's notes remained unresolved, as the parties had anticipated resolving the matter without further litigation.

Ms. Beerline reported that the settlement agreement was largely complete, but approval was still pending from a third party, Henry McKenzie, the brother of both James and Charles. She had not yet received confirmation from Henry or his counsel.

Given the long-standing delays and unresolved status, the Court ordered the matter continued to June 25, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and stated it would issue an Order to Appear for Henry McKenzie to encourage resolution and determine whether further action is necessary. The Court also indicated that it may consider issuing a Breslin Order at the next hearing, depending on Henry McKenzie's participation and progress in finalizing the settlement.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Continued To May-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
Off Calendar Apr-02-2025 Continued To May-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ross Spector appeared via zoom for and with James Mackenzie (Petitioner).
Frank Busch appeared via zoom for Charles Mackenzie (respondent).

Discussion was held regarding possible approval of a settlement, that currently there's a property under foreclosure that cannot be delayed any further, and that parties will file a petition first and then seek to file a exparte to advance the hearing date. The matter is continued to May 14, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Loke. Not reported.

Petition: (1) To Remove Trustee, Appoint Temporary Trustee/Receiver And Suspend Power Of Trustee Pending Trial; (2) To Interpret Trust Provisions; (3) To Surcharge Trustee; (4) For Damages For Fraud And Breach Of Trustee'S Fiduciary Duties; (5) For An Accounting; (6) For Final Distribution And Termination Of Trust; And (7) For Attorneys' Fee
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Continued To Apr-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ross A. Spector for James R. McKenzie, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Frank Busch for Charles McKenzie, Respondent (via Zoom).
Martin Woods for Henry McKenzie (via Zoom).

Mr. Spector informed the court that the matter had been resolved through mediation approximately nine months ago. However, finalizing the settlement has been delayed due to its complexity. However, progress resumed recently, and he is reasonably confident that a settlement agreement will soon be ready for submission to the court.

Mr. Spector estimated that the draft could be completed within three weeks, followed by the filing of a petition for approval. Mr. Busch agreed but suggested that additional time may be required due to the need to obtain consents from multiple parties and to file a Guardian Ad Litem petition.

The court continued the matter to April 2, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for the settlement petition to be filed.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
